executiveSummary.text: Icon plc (ICLR) is now back in sync with the canonical DYOR HQ sheet after the metadata repair pass. The current stance is REDUCE with conviction 50/100, using $106.81 as the latest stored price reference where available.

businessModel.text: Icon plc serves drug developers through outsourced research and development services, so backlog quality, bookings and execution matter more than one quarter in isolation.

recentCatalysts.text: Large pharma outsourcing budgets, biotech funding conditions and book-to-bill trends are the main drivers.

thesisEvaluation.text: Base case: the core operating backdrop remains good enough to support the present reduce stance, but not strong enough to justify a looser risk view without better evidence. Bull case: execution and demand improve faster than expected. Bear case: operating or funding pressure worsens and compresses valuation further.

keyRisks.text: Biotech funding weakness can slow demand. Programme delays can defer revenue recognition. Large-client concentration can matter. Labour and delivery execution remain important. Pricing can soften if industry capacity rises.

risks.risk: Biotech funding weakness can slow demand.
Programme delays can defer revenue recognition.
Labour and delivery execution remain important.
Pricing can soften if industry capacity rises.

whoShouldOwn.text: Own: Investors who want outsourced pharma-services exposure. Avoid: Investors who need low sensitivity to biotech funding cycles.

recommendation.text: REDUCE - Conviction: 50/100. This rebuilt page is intentionally concise, but it is now internally coherent and aligned to the corrected ticker and company metadata.

entryExit.text: Keep position sizing consistent with a reduce stance until fuller company-specific analysis is refreshed.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via the DYOR HQ proprietary market data workflow and internal canonical mapping.
